action.reasoning: Observation: the best submitted checkpoint is still the 10-model raw LightGBM ensemble (ic_mean=0.057296). Target normalization and ranker objectives did not improve Pearson IC, while prediction transforms only produced small losses. Analysis: a remaining orthogonal low-risk change is to align the feature representation with the cross-sectional IC objective by normalizing Alpha158 features within each trading day at both train and predict time; this is common in Qlib-style processors and should not use future labels. Decision: extend predict.py to support saved per-day feature transforms, add a training script that trains the proven 10-model LightGBM ensemble on daily z-scored features and raw labels through 2021, smoke-test locally, and submit it if pipeline succeeds.
action.command: python train_feat_cs.py --out checkpoints/feat_csz_raw_v1 --num_models 10 --feature_transform cs_zscore --include_valid_train && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/feat_csz_raw_v1 --output local_pred_feat_csz_raw_v1.json
